universeMode.title: Tomodachi Life Living The Dream Food Guide
universeMode.subtitle: Feed Miis, learn their tastes, and keep island life moving with a reliable daily care loop.
universeMode.intro: Fresh Kingdom is the main food stop on the island, but feeding Miis is more than a simple item drop. Food helps you care for residents, uncover their likes and dislikes, and build a stronger daily routine around happiness, friendships, and island personality. Palette House also expands this system by letting you create custom food for residents or for display around the island.

items.whatItIs: The island food mart where Miis discover favorite eats and culinary curiosities.
items.whatPlayersCanDo: Buy food for residents and use it as part of their day-to-day care.

items.whatItIs: Miis react clearly to gifts and show what they do and do not like.
items.whatPlayersCanDo: Use each reaction to build a practical taste profile for every resident.

items.whatItIs: Food appears in social scenes, including conversation moments between residents.
items.whatPlayersCanDo: Combine chat moments with gift reactions to understand who loves what.

items.whatItIs: Giving food is a core care action, and a full happiness gauge leads to level-ups.
items.whatPlayersCanDo: Feed Miis regularly alongside clothes, treasures, quirks, and relationship support.

items.whatItIs: Palette House lets you create food and drinks for residents and island display.
items.whatPlayersCanDo: Use custom food as both a care tool and a visual theme asset for districts and homes.

communityCreations.title: Tomodachi Life Living The Dream Outfits
communityCreations.subtitle: Dress every resident with everyday looks, novelty fits, and custom pieces from shop and workshop flows.
communityCreations.intro: Where & Wear is the island's main clothing store, covering daily outfits and playful costume-style options. Clothing also ties directly to resident feedback, so styling is not only cosmetic. Some gifts are perfect, others miss, and that makes wardrobe planning personal and dynamic.

items.title: Where and Wear
items.body: Tomodachi Life Living The Dream Where and Wear is the primary clothing shop for routine attire and standout costume looks.
highlights: Useful for resident gifting and styling

items.title: Daily Specials
items.body: Tomodachi Life Living The Dream adds daily specials, expanding both total variety and individual pieces over time.

items.title: Resident Reactions
items.body: Tomodachi Life Living The Dream residents react to clothes you give them, adding feedback to every style decision.
highlights: Not every gift lands the same way
Turns outfit planning into active management

items.title: Custom Clothing at Palette House
items.body: Tomodachi Life Living The Dream Palette House supports a special custom clothing workflow for personalized island fashion.
highlights: Pairs with other handmade island content

items.title: Share Looks Locally
items.body: Tomodachi Life Living The Dream creations from Palette House can be shared with nearby friends over local wireless.

items.title: Hamster Costume
items.body: Tomodachi Life Living The Dream demo completion grants a Hamster Costume, and the same outfit is also obtainable in full-game play.

theIsland.title: Tomodachi Life Living The Dream Room Interiors
theIsland.subtitle: Refresh living spaces with T and C Reno and build better homes for every resident on the island.
theIsland.intro: T and C Reno is the core destination for room interiors and living-space updates. It lets you shape how each resident home feels, while roommate systems, placement tools, and exterior customization add broader housing context. Together, these tools make housing one of the strongest long-term customization layers in the game.

items.title: T and C Reno Home Supply
items.body: Tomodachi Life Living The Dream T and C Reno is the dedicated interiors shop for revamping rooms and living spaces.

items.title: Theme Room Variety
items.body: Tomodachi Life Living The Dream shows highly themed rooms, including spaces inspired by settings like supermarkets and libraries.
highlights: Makes each resident room feel distinct

items.title: Daily Interior Options
items.body: Tomodachi Life Living The Dream T and C Reno rotates options daily, so new interior opportunities roll in over time.

items.title: Roommates and Shared Homes
items.body: Tomodachi Life Living The Dream allows shared homes, with up to eight Miis in one setup, changing social interactions at home.
highlights: Shared homes create additional social moments

items.title: Interiors Versus Exteriors
items.body: Tomodachi Life Living The Dream separates indoor room design and outdoor home styling between T and C Reno and Palette House.
highlights: Clear split between indoor and outdoor systems
Pairs room style with exterior identity

myfaction.title: Tomodachi Life Living The Dream Palette House
myfaction.subtitle: Use Palette House to design pets, food, clothing details, exteriors, and island items with a hands-on workflow.
myfaction.intro: Palette House is the main creative workshop and one of the biggest identity systems in Tomodachi Life Living The Dream. It moves customization beyond fixed shop inventories by letting you create original assets for residents and for the island itself, then share those creations locally.

items.title: Enter the Workshop
items.description: Tomodachi Life Living The Dream Palette House is the dedicated workshop for original creations beyond standard shop catalogs.

items.title: Choose a Creation Type
items.description: Create pets, custom drinks, food, favorite TV shows, clothing details, house exteriors, ground tiles, and original island items.

items.title: Start from a Base or from Scratch
items.description: For food creation, begin from a blank slate or use plates, bowls, boxes, cans, or earlier creations as your base.

items.title: Decorate with Tools and Stamps
items.description: Creation tools support stamps and Mii faces, and finished designs can become reusable stamps for future projects.

items.title: Place, Gift, and Share
items.description: Give creations to residents, place them around the island, and exchange Palette House content with nearby friends over local wireless.

Rebalance households when conflict rate gets too high

faq.title: Tomodachi Life Living The Dream
faq.subtitle: Quick answers for Tomodachi Life Living The Dream players

questions.question: When does Tomodachi Life Living The Dream launch?
questions.answer: Tomodachi Life Living The Dream launches on April 16, 2026 for Nintendo Switch.

questions.question: Can I transfer progress from the Tomodachi Life Living The Dream demo?
questions.answer: Yes. Demo progress can transfer to the full release in supported regions.

questions.question: What is the core loop in Tomodachi Life Living The Dream?
questions.answer: You create Miis, manage relationships, develop island spaces, and personalize homes, items, and daily routines.

questions.question: Does Tomodachi Life Living The Dream support local sharing?
questions.answer: Yes. Local wireless supports sharing selected Miis and custom creations with nearby players.

questions.question: How many players can join Tomodachi Life Living The Dream?
questions.answer: Tomodachi Life Living The Dream supports up to 2 players.

questions.question: Where should I check official Tomodachi Life Living The Dream updates?
questions.answer: Use Nintendo's store page, Nintendo news posts, and official trailer uploads for the latest updates.
